item.title: Version 2.0.3
item.description:
What's New in 2.0.3

✨ New Features

  • Hear your Mac's audio — Remio now streams your Mac's sound to the phone, tablet, or computer you're connecting from, so videos, music, and calls come through on the device in your hands.

⚙️ Improvements

  • Ready the moment your Mac restarts — Remio now turns on "Open at login" automatically during setup, and after a reboot it gets itself ready and starts streaming on its own — no need to open the app or click its icon first to become reachable.

item.title: Version 2.0.2
item.description:
What's New in 2.0.2

🐛 Bug Fixes

  • Fixed an external display losing its colors after a session — In rare cases, an external monitor (such as a Studio Display) could come back in black-and-white after a remote session ended, and only replugging the cable would fix it. Remio now detects this automatically and restores your display's colors within seconds — no need to touch the cable.

item.title: Version 2.0.1
item.description:
What's New in 2.0.1

🐛 Bug Fixes

  • Connect to your MacBook right from the lock screen — When you close your MacBook's lid and connect from another device, you now see the lock screen and can type your password to unlock your Mac remotely. Before, the app would show a black screen and drop the connection, making a lid-closed Mac impossible to reach.
  • Open the lid and your screen is instantly back — If your Mac's screen ever stayed black after a remote session, simply opening the lid now brings it right back. No more wondering whether the display broke or needing to force-restart.

item.title: Version 2.0.0
item.description:
What's New in 2.0.0

✨ New Features

  • A beautifully redesigned Remio Host — Remio Host has a brand-new look built around one clean, calm window. Everything you need to connect a phone, tablet, or another computer is right there the moment you open it — the QR code, your Device ID, and PIN — with settings and your account just a click away. It's simpler and easier to use than ever.
  • Keep your Mac reachable with the lid closed — On a MacBook, Remio can now keep your Mac awake even when you close the lid, so you can connect to it from another device while you're away. It automatically pauses if your Mac gets warm or the battery runs low, and you can choose to keep it awake only while it's plugged in.
  • A guided first-time setup — A friendly new welcome flow gets you ready in about a minute: sign in, grant the two permissions Remio needs to share and control your screen, and choose whether Remio starts automatically at login and stays ready for you.

item.title: Version 1.9.7
item.description:
What's New in 1.9.7

⚙️ Improvements

  • Performance improvements — A smoother, more reliable hosting experience.

item.title: Version 1.9.6
item.description:
What's New in 1.9.6

⚙️ Improvements

  • Performance improvements — A smoother, more reliable hosting experience.

item.title: Version 1.9.4
item.description:
What's New in 1.9.4

⚙️ Improvements

  • Performance improvements — A smoother, more reliable hosting experience.

item.title: Version 1.9.3
item.description:
What's New in 1.9.3

  • Your external display comes back exactly as you left it — On a MacBook used with the lid closed and an external monitor, ending a session now returns your external display to the same resolution and brightness you had before connecting, and your built-in display correctly stays off until you open the lid.

item.title: Version 1.9.2
item.description:
What's New in 1.9.2

🐛 Bug Fixes

  • Your Mac no longer gets stuck on a black screen after a device disconnects — When a connected phone, tablet, or computer closed the app or dropped off mid-session, your Mac's display could go dark and stay that way until you restarted it. Your screen now returns to normal within a few seconds of the session ending.

item.title: Version 1.9.1
item.description:
What's New in 1.9.1

  • Stays reliably available to connect to — Your Mac keeps itself ready for incoming connections more dependably — including right after it wakes from sleep, after a brief network change such as plugging in a dock or toggling a VPN, and if the connection service restarts.
  • Faster, steadier reconnecting — When a connection briefly drops, your Mac re-establishes it faster, and no longer interrupts a session that's actually still healthy.
  • Fixed rare cases where your Mac could show as offline for a short time right after reconnecting, or stop accepting new connections until the app was restarted.

item.title: Version 1.8.2
item.description:
What's New in 1.8.2

⚙️ Improvements

  • Motion stays smooth, automatically — Your Mac now always keeps motion fluid when the network is under pressure, briefly softening sharpness instead of ever stuttering or dropping frames. It works this way everywhere now, with no quality setting to manage.

item.title: Version 1.8.0
item.description:
What's New in 1.8.0

✨ New Features

  • Your Mac shares a live preview to your devices — Your phone and tablet now show a real snapshot of this Mac's screen on their device list, so you always know which Mac you're about to connect to. It works whether or not your Mac is signed in to the same account as your phone — even a quick guest pairing shows the preview.

⚙️ Improvements

  • Refreshed menu bar icon — The Remio icon in your menu bar has a cleaner, monochrome look that blends in naturally with macOS, with a small green dot that lights up the moment your Mac is ready to accept a connection.

item.title: Version 1.7.8
item.description:
Bug Fixes

  • Fixed a session loop that could leave the app stuck reconnecting — When the relay temporarily lost track of your streaming session (after a relay restart or a brief network hiccup), Remio Host could repeatedly try to reattach to the same now-gone session, requiring a manual quit and relaunch. Remio Host now detects this and quietly clears the stale session, so the next start begins cleanly without any user action.

item.title: Version 1.7.5
item.description:
What's New in 1.7.5

⚙️ Improvements

  • Crisp picture from the very first frame on big displays — Connecting from a Studio Display 5K, Pro Display XDR, MacBook Pro 16", or a 4K external monitor used to start blurry and slowly sharpen over the first few seconds while quality climbed. Remio Host now picks the right bitrate up front based on the size of the screen you're viewing on, so the picture is sharp the instant the stream appears.
  • Smooth motion when the network briefly slows — When Wi-Fi hits a momentary slow patch, scrolling, cursor movement, and animations stay smooth instead of stuttering down to a few frames per second. The picture may soften for a moment, but motion never feels jerky.
  • Sharper streaming on larger Macs — MacBook Pro 16" in any scaled mode and 4K external monitors in default Retina mode now stream at full Retina sharpness instead of being downsized to fit a smaller envelope.

item.title: Version 1.7.4
item.description:
What's New in 1.7.4

⚙️ Improvements

  • Now runs on macOS 15 Sequoia and later — Remio Host previously required the very latest macOS 26 Tahoe to install. It now works on any Mac running macOS 15 Sequoia or newer, so a much wider range of Macs can act as your Remio Host without needing to upgrade the operating system first.

item.title: Version 1.7.3
item.description:
What's New in 1.7.3

🐛 Bug Fixes

  • Reliable on a Mac mini you're already screen-sharing into — Connecting to a Mac mini with Remio while another remote session was already open (such as macOS Screen Sharing from another Mac) could previously show a frozen or wrongly-sized picture. Remio Host now reliably captures your real desktop in that scenario, with dynamic resizing working as expected.
  • Stable across rapid reconnects — Fixed a rare edge case where disconnecting and immediately reconnecting could destabilize the Mac's display system. Sessions now stay reliable across many quick connect-and-disconnect cycles.
